summary: Authenticated users can start Stripe Checkout for the Pro subscription plan.

steps.mint_appwrite_jwt: AuthContext creates an Appwrite JWT for the current session before requesting checkout.
steps.verify_api_identity: /api/billing/checkout verifies the JWT with Appwrite before creating a Stripe session.
steps.upsert_local_user: The API creates or updates public.users from verified Appwrite account data before starting checkout.
steps.create_checkout_session: The API creates a Stripe subscription Checkout Session using STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_PRO_PRICE_ID, APP_URL, user metadata, and Stripe-hosted promotion code entry.
steps.redirect_to_stripe: The client navigates to the Stripe-hosted checkout URL returned by the server.
steps.sync_subscription_from_webhook: Stripe webhook events update local customer and subscription tables after checkout completes.

conditions: Unauthenticated or invalid JWT requests must return 401.
Unexpected checkout request bodies must return 400.
Checkout must only use the server-side configured Stripe Price ID.
Stripe Checkout may accept Stripe-managed promotion codes through the hosted checkout page.
Client-provided price IDs, plan statuses, customer IDs, and redirect domains must not be trusted.
Checkout redirect URLs must be validated as Stripe-hosted HTTPS URLs before use.
Checkout metadata must include the verified Appwrite user id and local SQL user id for webhook reconciliation.
